---
title: "[title]"
subtitle: 'DOCUMENT: SAR-yyyy-NNN-XX-v01'
author: '**From:** Felipe Figueiredo **To:** ___'
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    fig_caption: yes
    fig_height: 6
    fig_width: 6
    keep_md: yes
    number_sections: yes
    toc: yes
  pdf_document:
    number_sections: yes
    toc: yes
  word_document:
    fig_caption: yes
    fig_height: 6
    fig_width: 6
    keep_md: yes
    reference_docx: misc/style_SAR_en.docx
    toc: yes
toc-title: "Table of Contents"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
# ************************************************************
# Template: SAR_en.Rmd
# Version:  0.xx.x
# Author:   Felipe Figueiredo
# Created:  2025-11-14
#
# ************************************************************
# QC Date:  YYYY-MM-DD
# ************************************************************

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE)
options(scipen = 999)
library(pander)
library(knitr)
library(flextable)
library(officer)
library(here)

knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = here())
panderOptions('table.style', 'rmarkdown')
panderOptions('table.split.table', 160)

# Initialize counters for Table indices
ind.table.body <- 0
ind.table.app <- 0
```

---

\newpage

**[title]**

**Document version**

```{r history}
# Define each version as an atomic row
v1 <- c("01", "YYYY-MM-DD", "Initial version.")

# Bind rows together
# Using naming in rbind automatically sets the row labels if needed, 
# but here we just want a clean table.
# Convert to data frame and set names
history <- tibble::as_tibble(rbind(v1), .name_repair = "minimal")
colnames(history) <- c("Version", "Date", "Alterations")

# Render
knitr::kable(
  history,
  align = "ccl",
)
```

```{r results, include=FALSE}
# Load cached results (Table 1, Table 2, Plots, etc.)
# This ensures all analysis outputs are available when compiling the report.
# Source 96- will safely check for and load all objects.
source(here("02 scripts", "96_load_cached_results.R"), encoding = 'UTF-8')
```

---

# Abbreviations

# Context

## Objectives

[Analyst Note: Use the Primary/Secondary/Exploratory list format. The use of a table is common in the Statistical Analysis Plan (SAP) document, but the bullet list is preferred for clarity in the final report.]

- **Primary:** To do analysis on outcome 1
- **Secondary:** To do analysis on outcome 2
- **Exploratory:** to do analysis on outcome 3

# Methods

[Analyst Note: This methods section provides a minimal description for the convenience of the reader (design, outcomes, exposures, and a concise statistical methods paragraph). It is compatible with the SAP snippet, and covers that deliverable for BASIC packages. Higher packages will include a SAP as a separate document]

[Analyst Note: DOCUMENTATION TIER ALIGNMENT REQUIRED]

- BASIC/STANDARD: delete the mention to a SAP (not scoped)
- PREMIUM: keep the reference to the Full SAP

The full details of the data procedures, study design, and statistical analysis methods used in this report are described in the annex document **SAP-yyyy-NNN-XX-v01**.

## Study Design

[Analyst Note: High-level summary of the study design (e.g., RCT, Observational Cohort, Case-Control).]

## Exposures and Outcomes

[Analyst Note: High-level summary of the primary exposure(s) and the primary/secondary outcomes analyzed.]

## Statistical Analysis

This analysis was performed using statistical software `R` version `r getRversion()`.

# Results

## Study Population Flowchart

[Analyst Note: This section must contain the STROBE/CONSORT flow diagram illustrating the sequential application of inclusion/exclusion criteria defined in the Methods to reach the final analytic population.]

Figure
1
shows the application of all eligibility criteria to the study population.

```{r flow, fig.cap="**Figure 1** Flowchart of eligibility criteria."}
# Figure 1: Primary Outcome Descriptive Plot
knitr::include_graphics(here("04 outputs", "Flowchart.png"), error = FALSE)
```

## Baseline characteristics

```{r ind_baseline}
ind.table.body <- ind.table.body + 1
tab.baseline.ind <- ind.table.body
```

Table
`r tab.baseline.ind`
shows the baseline characteristics of the study population.

```{r tab.baseline}
tab.baseline.cap <- "Baseline characteristics of the study population."
# Table 1: Descriptive table (created by 21_output_descriptive_table.R)
# if (exists("tab.baseline")) {
  tab.baseline %>%
    render_table(
      table_index = tab.baseline.ind,
      caption     = tab.baseline.cap
      )
# }
```

## Primary Analysis

```{r ind.primary}
ind.table.body <- ind.table.body + 1
tab.primary.P1.ind <- ind.table.body
```

This section presents the results for the primary objective.

Figure
2
shows the distribution of
the primary outcome
in the study population.

```{r fig_primary_outcome_dist, fig.cap="**Figure 2** Distribution of the primary outcome in the study population."}
# Figure: Primary Outcome Descriptive Plot
knitr::include_graphics(here("04 outputs", "Primary1_Outcome.png"), error = FALSE)
```

Table
`r tab.primary.P1.ind`
shows the unadjusted and adjusted estimates for the
primary
analysis.

```{r tab_primary, results='asis'}
tab.primary.P1.cap <- "Primary outcome analysis."
# Table 2: Primary Analysis Table (created by 32_output_primary_table.R)
if (exists("tab.primary.P1")) {
    tab.primary.P1 %>%
    render_table(
      table_index = tab.primary.P1.ind,
      caption     = tab.primary.P1.cap
      )
}
```

Figure
3
shows the
adjusted estimates for the effect of
the primary outcome
analysis.

```{r fig_primary_model_predict, fig.cap="**Figure 3** Primary outcome effect plot."}
# Figure: Primary model prediction plot
knitr::include_graphics(here("04 outputs", "Primary1_Predict.png"), error = FALSE)
```

## Secondary Analysis 1

[Analyst Note: remove this section if not scoped]

```{r ind_secondary1}
ind.table.body <- ind.table.body + 1
tab.secondary.S1.ind <- ind.table.body
```

This section presents the results for the secondary objectives.

Figure
4
shows the distribution of
the secondary outcome
in the study population.

```{r fig_secondary1_outcome, fig.cap="**Figure 4** Distribution of the secondary outcome 1 in the study population."}
# Figure: Secondary Outcome 1 Descriptive Plot
knitr::include_graphics(here("04 outputs", "Secondary1_Outcome.png"), error = FALSE)
```

Table
`r tab.secondary.S1.ind`
shows the unadjusted and adjusted estimates for the
secondary
analysis.

```{r tab_secondary1, results='asis'}
tab.secondary.S1.cap <- "Secondary outcome 1 analysis."
# Table: Secondary Analysis 1 Table (created by 42_output_secondary_table.R)
if (exists("tab.secondary.S1")) {
  tab.secondary.S1 %>%
    render_table(
      table_index = tab.secondary.S1.ind,
      caption     = tab.secondary.S1.cap
      )
} else {
  pander(data.frame(Note = "N/A - Secondary analysis 1 not performed or not applicable to this project scope."))
}
```

Figure
5
shows the
adjusted estimates for the effect of
the secondary outcome
analysis.

```{r fig_secondary1_predict, fig.cap="**Figure 5** Secondary outcome 1 effect plot."}
# Figure: Secondary model 1 prediction plot
knitr::include_graphics(here("04 outputs", "Secondary1_Predict.png"), error = FALSE)
```

## Secondary Analysis 2

[Analyst Note: remove this section if not scoped]

```{r ind_secondary2}
ind.table.body <- ind.table.body + 1
tab.secondary.S2.ind <- ind.table.body
```

This section presents the results for the secondary objectives.

Figure
6
shows the distribution of
the secondary outcome
in the study population.

```{r fig_secondary2_outcome, fig.cap="**Figure x** Distribution of the secondary outcome 2 in the study population."}
# Figure: Secondary Outcome 2 Descriptive Plot
knitr::include_graphics(here("04 outputs", "Secondary2_Outcome.png"), error = FALSE)
```

Table
`r tab.secondary.S2.ind`
shows the unadjusted and adjusted estimates for the
secondary
analysis.

```{r tab_secondary2, results='asis'}
tab.secondary.S2.cap <- "Secondary outcome 2 analysis."
# Table: Secondary Analysis 2 Table (created by 42_output_secondary_table.R)
if (exists("tab.secondary.S2")) {
  tab.secondary.S2 %>%
    render_table(
      table_index = tab.secondary.S2.ind,
      caption     = tab.secondary.S2.cap
      )
} else {
  pander(data.frame(Note = "N/A - Secondary analysis 2 not performed or not applicable to this project scope."))
}
```

Figure
7
shows the
adjusted estimates for the effect of
the secondary outcome
analysis.

```{r fig_secondary2_predict, fig.cap="**Figure x** Secondary outcome 2 effect plot."}
# Figure: Secondary model 2 prediction plot
knitr::include_graphics(here("04 outputs", "Secondary2_Predict.png"), error = FALSE)
```

## Secondary Analysis 3

[Analyst Note: remove this section if not scoped]

```{r ind_secondary3}
ind.table.body <- ind.table.body + 1
tab.secondary.S3.ind <- ind.table.body
```

This section presents the results for the secondary objectives.

Figure
8
shows the distribution of
the secondary outcome
in the study population.

```{r fig_secondary3_outcome, fig.cap="**Figure x** Distribution of the secondary outcome 3 in the study population."}
# Figure: Secondary Outcome 3 Descriptive Plot
knitr::include_graphics(here("04 outputs", "Secondary3_Outcome.png"), error = FALSE)
```

Table
`r tab.secondary.S3.ind`
shows the unadjusted and adjusted estimates for the
secondary
analysis.

```{r tab_secondary3, results='asis'}
tab.secondary.S3.cap <- "Secondary outcome 3 analysis."
# Table: Secondary Analysis 3 Table (created by 42_output_secondary_table.R)
if (exists("tab.secondary.S3")) {
  tab.secondary.S3 %>%
    render_table(
      table_index = tab.secondary.S3.ind,
      caption     = tab.secondary.S3.cap
      )
} else {
  pander(data.frame(Note = "N/A - Secondary analysis 3 not performed or not applicable to this project scope."))
}
```

Figure
x
shows the
adjusted estimates for the effect of
the secondary outcome
analysis.

```{r fig_secondary3_predict, fig.cap="**Figure x** Secondary outcome 3 effect plot."}
# Figure: Secondary model 3 prediction plot
knitr::include_graphics(here("04 outputs", "Secondary3_Predict.png"), error = FALSE)
```

## Exploratory Analysis 1

[Analyst Note: remove this section if not scoped]

```{r ind.exploratory}
ind.table.body <- ind.table.body + 1
tab.exploratory.E1.ind <- ind.table.body
```

This section presents the results for any post-hoc or exploratory findings.

Figure
x
shows the distribution of
the exploratory outcome
in the study population.

```{r fig_exploratory_outcome_dist, fig.cap="**Figure x** Distribution of the exploratory outcome in the study population."}
# Figure x: Exploratory Outcome Descriptive Plot
knitr::include_graphics(here("04 outputs", "Exploratory1_Outcome.png"), error = FALSE)
```

Table
`r tab.exploratory.E1.ind`
shows the unadjusted and adjusted estimates for the
exploratory
analysis.

```{r tab_exploratory, results='asis'}
tab.exploratory.E1.cap <- "Exploratory analysis results."
# Table 4: Exploratory Analysis Table (created by 52_output_exploratory_table.R)
if (exists("tab.exploratory.E1")) {
  tab.exploratory.E1 %>%
    render_table(
      table_index = tab.exploratory.E1.ind,
      caption     = tab.exploratory.E1.cap
      )
} else {
  pander(data.frame(Note = "N/A - Exploratory analysis not performed or not applicable to this project scope."))
}
```

Figure
6
shows the
adjusted estimates for the effect of
the exploratory outcome
analysis.

```{r fig_exploratory_model_predict, fig.cap="**Figure 6** Exploratory outcome effect plot."}
# Figure 6: Exploratory model prediction plot
knitr::include_graphics(here("04 outputs", "Exploratory1_Predict.png"), error = FALSE)
```

## Sensitivity Analysis

[Analyst Note: remove this section if not scoped]

```{r ind_sensitivity}
ind.table.body <- ind.table.body + 1
tab.primary.P1.sens.ind <- ind.table.body
```

Sensitivity analysis was performed to verify the robustness of the primary findings.

Table
`r tab.primary.P1.sens.ind`
shows the estimates from the
sensitivity
analysis.

```{r tab_sensitivity, results='asis'}
tab.primary.P1.sens.cap <- "Sensitivity analysis for the primary outcome."
# Table 5: Sensitivity Analysis (created by 36_output_primary_sens_table.R)
if (exists("tab.primary.P1.sens")) {
  tab.primary.P1.sens %>%
    render_table(
      table_index = tab.primary.P1.sens.ind,
      caption     = tab.primary.P1.sens.cap
      )
} else {
  pander(data.frame(Note = "N/A - Sensitivity analysis not performed."))
}
```

# Observations and Limitations

# Conclusions

# References

[Analyst Note: DOCUMENTATION TIER ALIGNMENT REQUIRED]

- BASIC/STANDARD: delete the reference to the Full SAP (not scoped)
- PREMIUM: keep the reference to the Full SAP

- **SAP-yyyy-NNN-XX-v01** -- Statistical Analysis Plan: [title]

# Appendix

## Supplementary Descriptive Material

[Analyst Note: Use this section for descriptive figures and tables (e.g., initial variable distributions, correlation checks, baseline tables for subgroups) that support the report but are not essential to the main Results narrative.]

```{r fig_appendix_eda, fig.cap="**Figure A1** Distribution of Age in the study population, by Sex."}
# Figure A1: Example of an Exploratory Data Analysis Plot
knitr::include_graphics(here("04 outputs", "Age_by_Sex.png"), error = FALSE)
```

## Modeling Strategy and Diagnostics

### Model Validation and Diagnostics

[Analyst Note: Use this subsection to detail the model's appropriateness and assumption checks based on the stock diagnostic routine (e.g., collinearity (VIF), influence/Leverage, and goodness-of-fit). ]

[Analyst Note: DOCUMENTATION TIER ALIGNMENT REQUIRED]

- BASIC: Summarize the minimal checks (R2, Outliers, Critical VIF > 10).
- STANDARD/PREMIUM: Document model-specific assumption tests (e.g., Normality, Homoscedasticity, Goodness-of-Fit) and specialized checks (e.g., Cox PH) if applicable.
Describe the findings of the diagnostic plots (if generated internally via functions like autoplot() for residual analysis).

### Model Selection and Justification

[Analyst Note: If multiple models were tested (e.g., adjusted vs. raw, nested), use this subsection to provide the justification (e.g., based on R-squared, AIC, ANOVA, or clinical relevance) for the primary model presented in the Results section.]

[Analyst Note: DOCUMENTATION TIER ALIGNMENT REQUIRED]

- STANDARD: Provide the Draft SAP justification for model selection (e.g., based on ANOVA/R-squared or clinical relevance).
- PREMIUM (Diagnostic Audit): Document the decision-making process for assumption violations (e.g., choice of robust standard errors, outcome transformation) and any complex collinearity interpretations.

### Full Model Specification

```{r ind_app_full_model}
ind.table.app <- ind.table.app + 1
tab.primary.P1.full.ind <- paste0("A", ind.table.app)
```

Table
`r tab.primary.P1.full.ind`
shows the coefficients for all variables included in the
primary
model.

```{r tab_app_full_model, results='asis'}
tab.primary.P1.full.cap <- "Full model specification for the primary analysis."
# Table A1: Full Model Specification (created by 32_output_primary_table.R)
if (exists("tab.primary.P1.full")) {
  tab.primary.P1.full %>%
    render_table(
      table_index = tab.primary.P1.full.ind,
      caption     = tab.primary.P1.full.cap
    )
} else {
  pander(data.frame(Note = "N/A - Full model table not available."))
}
```

### Full Secondary Model Specification

[Analyst Note: remove this section if not scoped]

```{r ind_app_full_secondary_model}
ind.table.app <- ind.table.app + 1
tab.secondary.S1.full.ind <- paste0("A", ind.table.app)
```

Table
`r tab.secondary.S1.full.ind`
shows the coefficients for all variables included in the
secondary
model.

```{r tab_app_full_secondary_model, results='asis'}
tab.secondary.S1.full.cap <- "Full model specification for the secondary analysis."
# Table A2: Full Secondary Model Specification (created by 42_output_secondary_table.R)
if (exists("tab.secondary.S1.full")) {
  tab.secondary.S1.full %>%
    render_table(
      table_index = tab.secondary.S1.full.ind,
      caption     = tab.secondary.S1.full.cap
      )
} else {
  pander(data.frame(Note = "N/A - Full model table for secondary analysis not available."))
}
```

### Full Exploratory Model Specification

[Analyst Note: remove this section if not scoped]

```{r ind_app_full_exploratory_model}
ind.table.app <- ind.table.app + 1
tab.exploratory.E1.full.ind <- paste0("A", ind.table.app)
```

Table
`r tab.exploratory.E1.full.ind`
shows the coefficients for all variables included in the
exploratory
model.

```{r tab_app_full_exploratory_model, results='asis'}
tab.exploratory.E1.full.cap <- "Full model specification for the exploratory analysis."
# Table A3: Full Exploratory Model Specification (created by 52_output_exploratory_table.R)
if (exists("tab.exploratory.E1.full")) {
  tab.exploratory.E1.full %>%
    render_table(
      table_index = tab.exploratory.E1.full.ind,
      caption     = tab.exploratory.E1.full.cap
    )
} else {
  pander(data.frame(Note = "N/A - Full model table for exploratory analysis not available."))
}
```

## Associated analyses

[Analyst Note: This section should be removed from the template for first projects. Uncomment and fill out if the project is part of a larger series.]

This analysis is part of a larger project and is supported by other analyses, linked below.

**[assoc_title]**

<[assoc_link]>

## Availability

All documents from this consultation were included in the consultant's Portfolio.

<!-- The client has requested that this analysis be kept confidential until a future date, determined by the client. -->
<!-- All documents from this consultation are therefore not published online and only the title and year of the analysis will be included in the consultant's Portfolio. -->
<!-- After the agreed date is reached, the documents will be released. -->

<!-- The client has requested that this analysis be kept confidential. -->
<!-- All documents from this consultation are therefore not published online and only the title and year of the analysis will be included in the consultant's Portfolio. -->

The portfolio is available at:

<https://philsf-biostat.github.io/SAR-yyyy-NNN-XX/>

## R Environment and Reproducibility

To ensure the reproducibility of these results, the following R packages and system environment details were used for the analysis and report generation.

```{r session_info}
sessionInfo()
```
